                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No.6545/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  08-07-2021
in CRR No. 1005/2012 passed by the High Court of Jharkhand at
Ranchi]
ASFAK ANJUM @ ASFAQUE ANJUM @ DULARE KHAN          Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF JHARKHAND & ANR.                      Respondent(s)
 
WITH
SLP(Crl) No. 6546/2026 (II-A)
SLP(Crl) No. 8645/2026 (II-A)
Date : 15-07-2026 These petitions were called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Mr. Mohd. Faisal, AOR
                   Mr. Mohd Faraheem Khan, Adv.
                   Mr. Saiful Islam, Adv.                   
For Respondent(s) : 
                   Ms. Madhusmita Bora, AOR
                   Ms. Pavithra V., Adv.                   
                   
                   Mr. Anando Mukherjee, AOR
                   Mr. Shwetank Singh, Adv.
                   Ms. Riya Dhingra, Adv.
                   Mr. Utkarsh Anand, Adv.                   
                   
                   Mr. Rana S Biswas, Adv.
                   Mr. Kartik Chettiar, Adv.
                   Mr. Yash Tripathi, Adv.
                   Mr. Sunil Kumar Sharma, AOR                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner is convicted of the offence punishable under
Section 138 of Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881. Respondent no. 2
is  the  original  complainant  and  had  lodged  three  separate

--- Page 408 ---
2
complaints in connection with three cheques purported to have been
issued by the petitioner in the name of Respondent No.2 when those
came to be dishonored for want of insufficient funds.
2. The petitioner was tried and convicted in the three cases
arising from the abovementioned complaints, which was upheld by the
Sessions Court and the High Court. The petitioner was sentenced to
two years simple imprisonment (S.I.) and compensation of double the
amount  of  the  cheques  dishonoured  was  awarded  with  default
sentence. 
3. One  SLP  arises  from  Complaint  Case  No.  1586/2006  (T.R.
No.516/2012), and the two connected SLPs arise out of Complaint
Case  No.1876/2006  (T.R.  No.531/2012)  and  Complaint  Case  No.
1875/2006 (T.R. No.524/2012) respectively. The revision petitions
filed against these orders were dismissed by the High Court, which
orders are impugned in the SLPs.
4. The learned Counsel for the petitioner has submitted that the
petitioner has deposited double the amount of the three cheques
before the Judicial First Class Magistrate’s Court, Dhanbad, as
ordered by the said Court. The second respondent would be entitled
to withdraw the amounts so deposited with the Magistrate’s Court
with any interest accrued thereon and the impugned Orders of the
learned  Judicial  First  Class  Magistrate’s  Court,  Dhanbad  as
affirmed by the High Court shall stand modified insofar as the
sentence being modified as till the rising of the Court affirming
compensation as ordered being double the amount of the cheques. The
bail bonds executed by the petitioner herein shall stand cancelled
on the petitioner’s appearance before Court, at the rising of the
Court.   
5.  The learned counsel further submitted that two other revision
petitions, namely, Criminal Revision Petition No. 469 of 2026
arising out of Complaint Petition No. 1587 of 2006, and Criminal
Revision Petition No. 474 of 2026 arising out of Complaint Petition
No. 1575 of 2006 filed by the Petitioner in relation to two other
cheques dishonoured were also dismissed by the High Court of
Jharkhand on 19.06.2026.

--- Page 409 ---
3
6.  We direct the Petitioner to deposit, before the Judicial First
Class Magistrate’s Court, Dhanbad compensation equivalent to twice
the aggregate amount of the aforesaid two cheques within a period
of two months from today, the compensation being modified to that
extent.  Upon  such  deposit,  the  amount  may  be  disbursed  to
Respondent No. 2. The substantive sentence of imprisonment, if any,
imposed shall stand modified as till rising of the Court. On
appearance of the petitioner before Court, on the rising of the
Court, the proceedings against the Petitioner shall stand closed
and disposed of. The bail bonds against the petitioner - herein in
those cases shall also then stand cancelled.
7. The above SLPs filed by the petitioner are disposed of as above
with further directions as to the two complaints filed by the
Respondent No.2 as mentioned above.
8. Pending applications, if any, shall stand disposed of.
 (VISHAL ANAND)                                    (POOJA SHARMA)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                                 COURT MASTER (NSH)

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CRIMINAL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CRIMINAL APPEAL NO.        /2026
  [@ SLP [CRL.] NO.8196/2026]
SARFARAZ @ MUZAFFAR AHMAD WANI          Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
THE UNION OF INDIA                      Respondent(s)
O R D E R
Leave granted.
The appellant seeks bail in connection with
Special  Case  No.  10  of  2024/R.C.  No.  17  of
2024/NIA/DLI arising out of Banjariya P.S. Case No.
229 of 2024 dated 05.09.2024 registered for the
offences punishable under Sections 317(5), 318(4),
61(2), 338, 336(3), 340(2), 111, 178, 179, 180, 181
and 3(5) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 and
Sections 16, 18 and 20 of the Unlawful Activities
(Prevention) Act, 1967.
We are not inclined to go into the merits of the
case at this juncture because the issue pertaining to
the non-furnishing of written grounds of arrest to
the appellant, though raised, has not been considered
by the High Court.
In such view of the matter, we set aside the
impugned order by remitting it to the High Court to
consider the said issue while deciding the bail

--- Page 417 ---
2
application of the appellant.
Appropriate orders will have to be passed within
a period of six weeks from the date of receipt of a
copy of this order.
All issues are left open, as we feel that the
interest of justice requires and it will only be
appropriate  if  the  matter  is  reheard  afresh,
including the issues already dealt with by the High
Court.
The appeal stands allowed accordingly.
Pending  application(s),  if  any,  shall  stand
disposed of.

[M.M. SUNDRESH]
...................J.
[PRASANNA B. VARALE]

Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.) No.8337/2026
[Arising  out  of  impugned  final  judgment  and  order  dated

passed by the High Court of Delhi at New Delhi]
FAIZAAN TAK                                     Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS

Date : 16-07-2026 This petition was called on for hearing 
today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Adarsh Kumar Tiwari, AOR
                   Mr. Vartika Maurya, Adv.
                   Ms. Akanksha Raj Mishra, Adv.
                   Mr. Satyendra Kumar Chauhan, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Rajkumar Bhaskar Thakare, A.S.G.
                   Mr. Shreekant Neelappa Terdal, AOR
                   Mr. Ishaan Sharma, Adv.
                   Ms. Prerna Dhal, Adv.
                   Ms. Tusharika Sharma, Adv.

                   Mr. Alankrit Bhatnagar, Adv.
                   Mr. Shashank Tiwari, Adv.
SLP(Crl.) No.8337/2026     1

--- Page 420 ---
                   Mr. Akshay Srivastava, AOR
                   
                   
UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
O R D E R
1. The petitioner stood trial for offences under Sections
450 and 376 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 (IPC) and was
convicted and sentenced to undergo simple imprisonment for
a period of seven years and fine of Rs.1,00,000/- and in
default of payment of fine, simple imprisonment for one
month.
2. The petitioner is challenging the order and judgment
of the Delhi High Court dated 13.03.2026 by which Crl. M.
(Bail)  232/2026  in  Crl.  A.  1031/2024  filed  by  the
petitioner seeking suspension of sentence was dismissed by
the  High  Court.  The  petitioner  is  before  this  Court
challenging  the  impugned  order  pointing  out  that  the
prosecutrix cannot be termed a ‘sterling witness’.
3. We heard Mr.Adarsh Kumar Tiwari, learned AoR appearing
for the petitioner, Mr. Rajkumar Bhaskar Thakare, learned
ASG and Mr.Alankrit Bhatnagar, learned counsel appearing
for the respondents.
4. On a prima facie consideration, we are of the opinion
SLP(Crl.) No.8337/2026     2

--- Page 421 ---
that this is a fit case for suspending the sentence and
releasing the petitioner on bail, pending final disposal of
the appeal, especially considering that the petitioner is
in custody for more than two years as against the fixed
term of seven years having been imposed.
5. The sentence imposed on the petitioner is suspended
and he is ordered to be released on bail, subject to terms
and conditions that the court of Additional Sessions Judge
(Special Court-Rape cases), South District, Saket Courts,
New Delhi (the jurisdictional court) deems fit and proper
to impose.
6. The Special Leave Petition is disposed of.
7. Pending  application(s),  if  any,  shall  also  stand
disposed of. 
    (CHANDRESH)                          (POOJA SHARMA)  
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                 COURT MASTER (NSH)
SLP(Crl.) No.8337/2026     3

--- Page 422 ---

   UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                  O R D E R
Heard  learned  counsel
for the parties and perused the
material placed on record.

and circumstances of the case
and, in particular, the fact
that  the  sanction  has  been
declined as far as the public
servants are concerned  and as
the  similarly  situated  co-
accused have been granted bail
and the charge survives only in
respect of the  offences under

  SLP (Crl.)  No(s).  8546/2026                  2 

--- Page 424 ---
Sections  61(2),  318(4)  of
BNS), we are inclined to grant
bail to the petitioner.
Accordingly, we direct that
the petitioner be released on
bail,  on  such  terms  and
conditions as may be imposed by
the Trial Court, in connection
with Crime No. RC 0082025A0008
registered at  Police Station
CBI/ACB,  District  Bhopal,
Madhya Pradesh.
The Special Leave Petition

Item no.42
Service was complete on respondent no.2 on 13.05.2026. Ld.
Counsel, Mr Wyomesh Tripathi appearing on behalf of  Mr. Omkar
Jayant Deshpande, Advocate-on-record undertakes to appear for the
said respondent and seeks time to file vakalatnama and counter
affidavit. Vakalatnama be filed within one week. Counter affidavit
be filed within two weeks as last opportunity. If filed be taken on
record, else further opportunity to file the same would stand
declined.

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
Transfer Petition(s)(Civil)  No(s).  3672/2025
JAGRUTI BHATU PATIL    PETITIONER(S)
VERSUS
BHATU RAJENDRA PATIL    RESPONDENT(S)
ORDER
1. We have heard learned counsel for the petitioner-wife.  
None has appeared for the respondent-husband.
2. This Transfer Petition has been filed by the petitioner-
wife seeking transfer of FSUIT/1415/2025 ( Bhatu Rajendra  
Patil versus Jagruti Bhatu Patil ) filed by the respondent-
husband under Section 13(1)(i-a) of the Hindu Marriage Act,  
1955, pending before the Court of Principal Judge, Family  
Court at Surat, Gujarat to the Court of Principal Judge,  
Family Court at Pune, Maharashtra.
3. This Court vide order dated 06.01.2026 had issued notice 
and stayed further proceedings in FSUIT/1415/2025. Office  
report dated 14.07.2026 indicates that notice has been  
served upon the respondent-husband but none has entered  
appearance.
4. We have perused the grounds mentioned in the Transfer  
Petition and we are satisfied that a case has been made out  
for transfer of the proceedings.
5. Accordingly, we direct that the entire case record of  
FSUIT/1415/2025 shall be forthwith transferred from the  
1

--- Page 483 ---
Court of Principal Judge, Family Court at Surat, Gujarat to  
the  Court  of  Principal  Judge,  Family  Court  at  Pune,  
Maharashtra.
6. It would be open to the respondent-husband to file  
appropriate  application  before  the  transferee  Court  to 
appear virtually in the proceedings.
7. The Transfer Petition is allowed on the above terms.
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
…………………………………………………...J.
          [UJJAL BHUYAN]
…………………………………………………...J.
          [ATUL S. CHANDURKAR]
 NEW DELHI;
 15th JULY, 2026.
 AK

Court at Aligarh, Uttar Pradesh.
3. This Court vide order dated 12.12.2025 had issued notice 
and stayed further proceedings in F Suit No.3245 of 2024.  
Office report dated 14.07.2026 indicates that respondent-
husband refused to accept notice and hence, service is  
deemed to be complete.
4. We have perused the grounds mentioned in the Transfer  
Petition and we are satisfied that a case has been made out  
for transfer of the proceedings.
5. Accordingly, we direct that the entire case record of F 
Suit No.3245 of 2024 shall be forthwith transferred from the 
1

--- Page 486 ---
Court of  Judge, Family Court  6 at City Civil Compound,  
Ahmedabad, Gujarat  to the  Family Court at Aligarh, Uttar  
Pradesh.
6. It would be open to the respondent-husband to file  
appropriate  application  before  the  transferee  Court  to 
appear virtually in the proceedings.
7. The Transfer Petition is allowed on the above terms.
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.

Sessions Judge, Court No.0-01, Azamgarh. The charges  
against the petitioner are under Sections 302/120B of

course of trial, petitioner had filed an application  
before the Trial Court for summoning of a Doctor, who  
had prepared the medical report. That application was  
dismissed  by  the  Trial  Court  vide  order  dated  
11.05.2026.
4. Aggrieved thereby, petitioner had moved the High  
Court  of  Allahabad  (briefly  the  ‘High  Court’  
hereinafter) but from a perusal of the impugned order  
dated 02.07.2026, it is seen that the application was  
withdrawn by the petitioner.

to  the  petitioner  to  file  appropriate  
application/proceedings before the Court of competent  
2

--- Page 490 ---
jurisdiction at an appropriate stage.
6. On a query by the Court, learned counsel for the

now fixed for arguments. At this stage, unless there is 
a direction from this Court, the Trial Court would not  
consider such an application.
7. Having regard to the liberty granted by the High  
Court, we are of the view that it is open to the  
petitioner  to  move  an  appropriate  application  for 
examination of the concerned Doctor who had prepared  
the medical report. If such an application is filed  
within a period of two weeks from today, the same shall 
be considered on its own merit by the Trial Court,

        UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
 
1. Through the present petition the petitioner is seeking
anticipatory bail in connection with FIR No.57 of 2024,
registered at Police Station, Kaler, District Arwal,
Bihar for the offences under Sections 147/148/149/341/
323/332/353/188/290 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 (for
short, “the IPC”) and Sections 3/4/9 of the Bihar
Control of the Use and Play of Loudspeakers Act, 1955.
2. We  heard  Mr.Neeraj  Shekhar,  the  learned  counsel
appearing for the petitioner and Mr.Samir Ali Khan, the
learned counsel, appearing for the respondent.
3. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the
other co-accused have already been granted anticipatory
bail by the Patna High Court. The petitioner has no
prior  criminal  antecedents.  Further,  the  custodial
interrogation of the petitioner is not necessary since
no recovery is to be effected from the petitioner.
SLP(Crl.)  No.6252/2026 1

--- Page 493 ---
4. The  anticipatory  bail  application  filed  before  the
Sessions Court and the High Court were both rejected.
5. On an overall consideration of the material available on
record and after hearing the learned counsel for the
parties, we accept the plea for grant of anticipatory
bail.
6. In view of the aforesaid, this petition is allowed. We
direct that in the event of the arrest of the petitioner
by the Investigating Officer (I.O) in connection with
the Crime number referred to above, the petitioner shall
be released on bail subject to terms and conditions as
may be imposed by the I.O.
7. It is needless to clarify that for the purpose of
effective investigation the petitioner shall cooperate
with the I.O.  The petitioner shall also appear before
the Court as and when directed by the I.O.

                   Mr. Aniruddha Purushotham, AOR

        UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner has been denied anticipatory bail
by the High Court of Bombay vide judgment in ABA No.
522 of 2024 dated 23.03.2026 in connection with
First  Information  Report  bearing  No.003  of  2024
registered with Police Station Borivali, for the
offences punishable under Sections 323, 406, 498-A
and 504 read with 34 of the Indian Penal Code (for
short, “the IPC”).
2. The High Court rejected the petition on the
ground  that  the  petitioner  failed  to  return
streedhan and pay the amount of Rs. 18 lakhs to
respondent no. 2/applicant, as volunteered by him
vide order dated 26.02.2024.
3. We heard the learned counsel appearing for the
petitioner, the learned counsel appearing for the
State and the learned counsel appearing for the
second respondent/de-facto complainant.
4. Respondent  no.  2,  wife  is  the  original
complainant  who  alleges  that  the  petitioner  and
other co-accused, her in-laws have not returned her
ornaments,  documents  and  personal  articles.  The
SLP(Crl.)  No.7428/2026 2

--- Page 499 ---
present  dispute  is  predominantly  matrimonial  in
nature  and  arose  after  prolonged  matrimonial
discord. The parties were living separately since
June 2022 whereas FIR was registered on 03.01.2024.
5. In the facts and circumstances of this case, we are
of the view that the petitioner deserves to be granted
the benefit of anticipatory bail subject to his continued
cooperation in the investigation.
6. In the result, we order that in the event of arrest
of the petitioner in connection with the crime detailed
above, he shall be released on bail, subject to terms and
conditions that the Investigating Officer (I.O.) may deem
fit to impose. The petitioner shall appear before the
I.O.  as  and  when  summoned  and  before  the  Court  as
directed by the I.O.
7. With the aforesaid, this petition stands disposed of.
8. Pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.

UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
O R D E R
1. The petitioner stood trial for offences under Sections
450 and 376 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 (IPC) and was
convicted and sentenced to undergo simple imprisonment for
a period of seven years and fine of Rs.1,00,000/- and in
default of payment of fine, simple imprisonment for one
month.
2. The petitioner is challenging the order and judgment
of the Delhi High Court dated 13.03.2026 by which Crl. M.
(Bail)  232/2026  in  Crl.  A.  1031/2024  filed  by  the
petitioner seeking suspension of sentence was dismissed by
the  High  Court.  The  petitioner  is  before  this  Court
challenging  the  impugned  order  pointing  out  that  the
prosecutrix cannot be termed a ‘sterling witness’.
3. We heard Mr.Adarsh Kumar Tiwari, learned AoR appearing
for the petitioner, Mr. Rajkumar Bhaskar Thakare, learned
ASG and Mr.Alankrit Bhatnagar, learned counsel appearing
for the respondents.
4. On a prima facie consideration, we are of the opinion
SLP(Crl.) No.8337/2026     2

--- Page 502 ---
that this is a fit case for suspending the sentence and
releasing the petitioner on bail, pending final disposal of
the appeal, especially considering that the petitioner is
in custody for more than two years as against the fixed
term of seven years having been imposed.
5. The sentence imposed on the petitioner is suspended
and he is ordered to be released on bail, subject to terms
and conditions that the court of Additional Sessions Judge
(Special Court-Rape cases), South District, Saket Courts,
New Delhi (the jurisdictional court) deems fit and proper
to impose.
6. The Special Leave Petition is disposed of.
7. Pending  application(s),  if  any,  shall  also  stand
disposed of. 
    (CHANDRESH)                          (POOJA SHARMA)  
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                 COURT MASTER (NSH)

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CRIMINAL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
Criminal Appeal No(s).  3199/2026
SLP (Crl.) No(s). 8772/2026
SARWAN KUMAR                                       Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF HARYANA                                   Respondent(s)
O R D E R 
1. Leave granted.
2. Heard  learned  counsel  for  the
parties.
3. This  appeal  arises  from  an  order
dated 09.03.2026 passed by the High Court of
Punjab and Haryana at Chandigarh rejecting
the regular bail prayer of the appellant in
connection  with  FIR  No.  742/2023,
registered at P.S. City Ballabgarh, District
Faridabad. 
4. Having regard to the fact that the
offences for which the appellant is being
tried  are  all  triable  by  a  Court  of
Magistrate and by now investigation is over
and  the  appellant  has  already  suffered
1

--- Page 507 ---
incarceration  for  a  period  exceeding  8
months,  we  are  of  the  view  that  the
appellant is entitled to be released on bail
at this stage.
5. Accordingly, the appeal is allowed.
The order passed by the High Court rejecting
the regular bail prayer of the appellant is
set aside.
6. The appellant shall be released on
bail on such terms and conditions as the
trial court may deem fit to impose in the
facts and circumstances of the case.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall
stand disposed of.

ITEM NO.1               COURT NO.6               SECTION II-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  Nos.7791-
7792/2026
[Arising  out  of  impugned  final  judgment  and  order  dated
16-04-2024 in IA No. 1031/2024 in Cr.Appeal (DB)No.1878/2023
22-07-2025 in IA No. 6704/2025 in Cr.Appeal (DB)No.1878/2023
passed by the High Court of Jharkhand at Ranchi]
UTTAM KUMAR RAJAK                                Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF JHARKHAND                              Respondent(s)
IA No. 56114/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
IA No. 98216/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL 
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 16-07-2026 These matters were called on for hearing 
today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Rakesh Kumar, Adv.
                   Ms. Priyanka Kumari, Adv.
                   Mr. Satish Kumar, Adv.
                   Mr. Chand Qureshi, AOR
                   Ms. Davinder Pal Kaur, Adv.
                   Mr. Abhishek Gurawa, Adv.
                   
For Respondent(s) : Ms. Pallavi Langar, AOR
SLP(Crl.)  Nos.7791-7792/2026 1Cr.

--- Page 510 ---
                   
        UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                           
O R D E R
1. The petitioner, who is the 6th Accused, is in custody
since 13.05.2021, having been convicted under Section
364A read with Section 34 of Indian Penal Code (“IPC”),
and sentenced to life imprisonment by the Sessions
Court with appropriate fine and default sentence.  Five
of  the  other  co-accused  have  been  granted  bail
suspending their sentence including the 1st Accused
Rupesh – from whose mobile the ransom call was made.
2. The present petitions arise from the impugned judgments
dated 16.04.2025 and 22.07.2025 whereby the High Court
rejected IA No. 1031 of 2024 in Cr. Appeal (DB) 1878 of
2023 and IA No. 6704 of 2025 in Cr. Appeal (DB) 1878 of
2023, filed for suspension of sentence.
3. The petitioner is now before us seeking grant of
regular bail and suspension of sentence in FIR No.32 of
2021 dated 12.05.2021.
4. We heard Mr. Rakesh Kumar, learned counsel appearing
for the petitioner and Ms. Pallavi Langar, learned AoR
SLP(Crl.)  Nos.7791-7792/2026 2Cr.

--- Page 511 ---
appearing for the respondent-State.
5. The sentence of the other accused has been suspended
finding the identification to be doubtful since the
wife of the victim admitted that the accused were shown
to her before the Test Identification Parade. The
victim also admitted that he was blindfolded when the
entire episode took place.
6. On a prima facie consideration, we are of the opinion
that the sentence of the Petitioner can be suspended
pending appeal(s).
7. The sentence imposed on the Petitioner is suspended and
he  shall  be  released  on  bail,  on  such  terms  and
conditions the trial court may deem fit to impose.
8. With the aforesaid, these petitions stand disposed of.
9. Pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.
(CHANDRESH)                                    (POOJA SHARMA)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                      COURT MASTER (NSH)
SLP(Crl.)  Nos.7791-7792/2026 3Cr.

--- Page 512 ---
ITEM NO.2               COURT NO.6               SECTION II-E
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No.6410/2026
[Arising  out  of  impugned  final  judgment  and  order  dated
20-01-2026 in WA No. 155/2026 passed by the High Court of
Madhya Pradesh at Indore]
PREM SINGH                                     Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF M.P & ANR.                           Respondent(s)
IA No. 109400/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED
JUDGMENT
 
Date : 16-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Vaibhav Srivastava, Adv.
                   Ms. Sugandha Anand, AOR
                   Mr. Chetan Anand Mohapatra, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) :Ms. Manisha T. Karia, Sr. A.A.G.
                   Mr. Abhinav Srivastav, Adv.
                   Mr. Pashupathi Nath Razdan, AOR
                   
                   
       UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
SLP(Crl.)  No.6410/2026                                           1

--- Page 513 ---
                             O R D E R
1. The Petitioner sought extension of parole on medical
grounds, specifically for his treatment for Carcinoma
Prostate with Bone Metastasis. The Petitioner is now
suffering also from complications arising after surgery.
2. The Petitioner was convicted by Sessions Court Byabra,
District  Rajgarh,  MP  for  offences  punishable  under
Sections 147, 148, 342, 302 read with 149, and 323 read
with 140 of Indian Penal Code, 1860 (for short “the IPC”)
vide  judgment  dated  19.12.2001  in  Sessions  Case  No.
12/2000 and has been inter alia imposed with a life
sentence.
3. The petitioner approached the High Court through a Writ
Petition  and  was  allowed  special  parole  between  the
period from 08.08.2025 to 10.12.2025 which was extended
from time to time on medical grounds. Madhya Pradesh High
Court in order dated 10.10.2025 in WP No.40084/2025 had
extended the parole for a period of 2 months with the
stipulation that no further extension or parole under any
circumstance shall be granted to the Petitioner.
4. The Petitioner is 79 years old and has been receiving
treatment from Chirayu Cancer Hospital. He submits that
SLP(Crl.)  No.6410/2026                                           2

--- Page 514 ---
his condition is an advanced and life-threatening stage
of  cancer,  which  requires  continuous  and  specialized
medical  treatment.  Further,  he  submits  that  he  has
already served 16 years 5 months and 11 days of his
sentence, with remission.
5. We are of the opinion that the age and physical condition
of the Petitioner makes this a fit case for grant of
continuance on parole to the Petitioner. The Petitioner
shall  not  hence  be  directed  to  surrender  and  shall
continue on parole.
6. The State shall consider his case for remission and in
the meanwhile, the learned counsel for the State shall
produce a report of the conduct of the Petitioner during
his incarceration, before this Court. The Registry shall
forward  the  above  order  to  the  appropriate  State
authorities for consideration of remission.
7. The matter shall be posted on 26.10.2026.
(CHANDRESH)                              (POOJA SHARMA)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                  COURT MASTER (NSH)
SLP(Crl.)  No.6410/2026                                           3

                            O R D E R
Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and learned Senior  
Counsel for the respondent No.2, as also the husband and son of the  
petitioner, who are present in the Court along with the respondent  

--- Page 537 ---
No.2.
2. The Court had detailed interaction with all the parties  
including the learned Senior counsel/counsel for them. The Court  
finds that the parties have many grievances against each other and  
at the beginning, it appeared that the issues could not be sorted  
out. However, after some time, suggestion came from Mr. Salman  
Khurshid, learned Senior counsel that the parties may be referred  
to counseling, which was accepted by learned counsel for the  
petitioner, Ms. Kamini Jaiswal.
3. Since the Court could not arrive at a decision with regard to  
what could be the next step forward, as has been suggested by  
learned counsel for the parties, the matter is adjourned to  
20.07.2026 to be taken up in Chambers at 2.00 p.m.
4. On that day, we are confident that learned counsels in  
consultation with their clients will be able to come out with a way  
forward and suggest names of the person(s) to whom they should be  
referred for counseling.
5. In the meantime, we request the husband of the petitioner and  
the respondent No.2 to have a relook at the stand they have been  
taking till now so that the ultimate goal of ensuring that the son  
of the petitioner lives a normal life, at least henceforth, is  
achieved, without carrying any ill will or baggage from the past.
6. We also request to learned counsel to advise their clients  
respectively and also to arrange for a meeting with all the parties  
along with their learned counsels.
7. We indicate that the husband of the petitioner may be working  
in  Singapore,  but  we  are  confident  that  the  employer  would  
understand that these proceedings are important, and at the request  

--- Page 538 ---
of the Court, the husband of the petitioner has agreed to extend  
his leave.
(SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA)                       (ANJALI PANWAR)

ITEM NO.54               COURT NO.1               SECTION IV-C
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (C)  No(s).  23183/2026
[Arising out of impugned judgment and order dated 23-06-2026 in WP
No. 22400/2026 passed by the High Court of Madhya Pradesh Principal
Seat at Jabalpur]
SOM DISTILLERIES PVT. LTD.                         Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF MADHYA PRADESH & ORS.                 Respondent(s)
(IA No. 193422/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T. and IA No.
193421/2026  -  PERMISSION  TO  FILE  ADDITIONAL  DOCUMENTS/FACTS/
ANNEXURES)
 
Date : 15-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE THE CHIEF JUSTICE
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
         HON'BLE MRS. JUSTICE V. MOHANA
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Mukul Rohatgi, Adv.
                   Mr. Sumeer Sodhi, AOR
                   Mr. Kapil Wadhwa, Adv.
                   Mr. Arjun Nanda, Adv.
                   Ms. Ravina Rathore, Adv.
                   Ms. Apoorva Maheshwari, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : Mr. Sridhar Potaraju, A.A.G.
                   Ms. Mrinal Gopal Elker, AOR
                   Mr. Srajan Yadav, Adv.
                   
                   Mr. Vikas Singh, Sr. Adv.
Ms. Shweta Singh Parihar, AOR
Mr. Kshitij Padhi, Adv.
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We  have  heard  learned  senior  counsel  on  behalf  of  the
petitioner, learned AAG for the State of Madhya Pradesh and another
private entity, who sought to oppose the petition.
1

--- Page 540 ---
2. It seems that the petitioner has two types of grievances:
(i) against the order dated 18.06.2026 whereby its application for
renewal of various liquor licences was rejected, and
(ii) against the Notice Inviting Tender (NIT) dated 12.06.2026
issued by the Excise Commissioner.  The petitioner’s grievance
seems to be against the tender conditions requiring a valid licence
as one of the conditions to be eligible to apply in response to
NIT.
3. Both these grievances were raised in a common writ petition
bearing No.22400/2026.
4. It further seems that, as per the roster management of the
High Court of Madhya Pradesh, tender matters are listed before a
Division Bench whereas the matter re: renewal of liquor licences is
listed before a Single Bench.
5. Since, in the writ petition, the tender conditions of NIT
dated 12.06.2026 were also challenged, the writ petition came up
before a Division Bench of the High Court and vide order dated
23.06.2026, the High Court declined to interfere with respect to
relief Nos.(i), (ii), (iii) and (iv) of the writ petition primarily
on the ground that in the absence of a valid liquor licence, the
petitioner is not eligible to apply against the NIT and that its
tender came to be rejected on the same date when the matter was
heard by the Division Bench. So far as the issue which was to be
decided by the learned Single Judge, in terms of paragraph 16 of
the impugned order, the learned Division Bench relegated the matter
before the Single Judge to decide the relief Nos.(v), (vi) and
(vii) of the writ petition.
2

--- Page 541 ---
6. In our considered view, it would have been appropriate for the
Division Bench of the High Court to decide all the issues through a
composite judgment. This is also necessitated in view of the nature
of the reliefs sought by the petitioner. Consequently, we modify
paragraph 16 of the impugned order insofar as it relegates the writ
petition to a Single Judge regarding relief Nos. (v), (vi) and
(vii) of the writ petition.  Consequently, we request the Division
Bench of the High Court to take up the petitioner’s writ petition
qua these reliefs also and pass appropriate orders after hearing
the parties. It will be appreciated if the High Court may pass
appropriate orders as early as possible and preferably within two
weeks.
7. The petitioner shall be at liberty to amend the writ petition
within three days.
8. Since we have not gone into the merits of the case, all the
contentions raised on behalf of the parties are left open to be
raised before the High Court.
9. The Special Leave Petition is, accordingly, disposed of with
pending application(s), if any.
(NITIN TALREJA)                            (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        DY. REGISTRAR
3

--- Page 542 ---
IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL ORIGINAL JURISDICTION
Transfer Petition(s)(Civil)  No(s).  989/2026
ANJALI M. MIRAJKAR @ TANISHA                       Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
MEGHARAJA MIRAJKAR                                 Respondent(s)
O R D E R
1. This is a petition filed under Section 25 of the Code of
Civil Procedure, 1908. The petitioner has filed this Transfer
Petition seeking the following reliefs:
“Transfer the proceedings bearing M.C. No. 252/2025,
titled as “Sri Megharaja Mirajkar vs. Smt. Anjali M.
Mijarkar @ Tanisha”, pending before the Family Court,
Shivamogga, Karnataka, to the Family Court, Solapur,
Maharashtra.”
2. Having heard the learned counsels for the petitioner and on
perusal of the material on record, we find that the petitioner has
made out a case for transfer of the aforesaid petition.
3. In the result, the Transfer Petition is allowed and M.C.
No. 252/2025, titled as “Sri Megharaja Mirajkar vs. Smt. Anjali M.
Mijarkar @ Tanisha”, pending before the Family Court, Shivamogga,
Karnataka, is hereby transferred to the Family Court, Solapur,
Maharashtra.
1

--- Page 543 ---
4. Registry to communicate this order to the transferor court
for taking steps in accordance with this order.
5. On an application made by the respondent, the Court to
which the case is transferred shall permit the respondent to appear
through video-conference on all dates except on the dates on which
his personal presence is absolutely mandatory.
6. The transfer petition stands allowed in the aforesaid
terms.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
...................J
(ARAVIND KUMAR)
..................J.
(VIPUL M. PANCHOLI)
NEW DELHI;
July 14, 2026.

SLP(C) No. 24361-24364/2023
Having heard learned senior counsels appearing for the parties
and having regard to the Order dated 3.11.2023, we are of the
considered view that sufficient time has passed and yet the matter
has not been disposed of. Hence, in order to balance the equities,
we direct the petitioner-Jagaran Prakashan Limited to deposit the
award amount insofar as the respondent No. 1(Amar Kumar Singh) and
respondent No. 2(Kishan Lal) are concerned.
 In the peculiar facts of the instant case we also make it
explicitly clear that this order to deposit the amount by the
2

--- Page 550 ---
petitioner would not be treated or considered as a concession on
the part of the petitioner and it shall not have any binding effect
in any of the pending proceedings.
On such award amount being deposited within an outer limit of
four weeks from today, Registry shall ensure that it is kept in a
short term Fixed Deposits and it is needless to state that
successful party would be eligible or entitled to receive the same
with accrued interest thereon. The prayer sought for by the
respondent(s)’ workman for release of the amount stands rejected at
this stage.
SLP(C) No. 10629/2026
Learned counsel for the petitioner(s) prays for and is granted
three weeks’ time to file rejoinder affidavit.
List all the matters for hearing after three weeks.

1. IA No.171850/2026 is rejected.

we do not find any justifiable reason to entertain the Review
Petition. The Review Petition is, accordingly, dismissed.
3. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.

                             O R D E R
1. Heard Mr. Ashok Kumar Panigrahi, learned counsel
appearing  for  the  petitioner  and  Mr.  Jayant  Mohan,
learned counsel appearing for the respondent-State.
2. The  present  Special  Leave  Petition  calls  in
question the correctness of the order dated 6 th April,
2026 passed by the High Court of Jharkhand at Ranchi in
anticipatory Bail Application No.1249 of 2026 whereby the
1

--- Page 558 ---
anticipatory bail application of the petitioner has been
dismissed.
3. When the matter came up for hearing on 6 th May,
2026, this Court while issuing notice, granted interim
protection to the petitioner.
4. We have considered the facts and circumstances,
including the joint compromise petition filed at Annexure
P-3 (page 29 pdf 45).  Being a petition for anticipatory
bail, we are not delving deep into the facts of the case.
5. Considering the overall facts and circumstances and
the fact that the petitioner has already been given
interim protection, we confirm the order dated 6 th May,
2026 granting interim protection to the petitioner.
6. The petitioner shall however cooperate with the
investigation.
7. The Special Leave Petition is disposed of in the
above terms.
8. Pending application shall also stand disposed of.

For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The submission on behalf of the petitioners is that
the Order dated 04.02.2026 in Civil Appeal No.758 of 2026
1

--- Page 598 ---
makes a categoric declaration that the appointment, whether
part time or contractual, which has continued for over 10
years, would be deemed to be permanent. Besides there is a
direction to pay emoluments etc. at the rate specified
therein. It is the case of the petitioners that despite the
aforesaid  declaration,  the  respondents  are  treating  the
appointment  of  the  petitioners  as  contractual  and  have
issued notice for renewal of the contract for a fresh period
of 11 months. According to the petitioners, such action of
the respondents is contemptuous and in teeth of this Court’s
judgment and order dated 04.02.2026.
2. At this stage, we deem it appropriate to issue
notice only to respondent nos.2, 3 and 4 in both the
Contempt Petitions, returnable in six weeks. 
3. Unless directed otherwise, the personal presence of
the respondents is not required.

                               O R D E R
1. The  petitioner  challenges  the  judgment/order  dated
19.03.2026 in CRM-M No.7635/2026 passed by the High Court
of Punjab & Haryana at Chandigarh.
2. We have heard the learned counsel for the parties and
perused the material available on record.
3. Having considered the submissions advanced across the
1

--- Page 602 ---
Bar, we are not inclined to interfere with the impugned
judgment/order.
4. Learned counsel for the petitioner, under instructions,
submits that the petitioner shall surrender on or before
17.08.2026 and move an application for the grant of regular
bail. Ordered accordingly.
5. We hope and request the concerned Court to consider and
decide  such  application  on  an  expeditious  basis,
uninfluenced by the observations made by the High Court in
the impugned judgment/order, as also the order passed by
this Court.
6. It shall be open for the petitioner to place the entire
material before the concerned Court, including the copy of
the video allegedly depicting as to what really transpired
at the place of occurrence.
7. Accordingly, the present Special Leave Petition stands
disposed of.
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of.

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 26-09-2025
in SBCRSMSOSA No. 878/2023 in S.B. Criminal Appeal (SB) No.
1456/2020 passed by the High Court of Judicature for Rajasthan at
Jaipur]
BALRAM                                             Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF RAJASTHAN                                 Respondent(s)
 
Date : 16-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KUMAR
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJEEV SACHDEVA
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Mr. Md. Ali, AOR
                   Ms. Shalu, Adv.                                 
For Respondent(s) : 
                   Mr. Padmesh Mishra, A.A.G.
                   Mr. S. Udaya Kumar Sagar, AOR                   
          UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
We find from the impugned order dated 26.09.2025 that the
Division Bench of the High Court of Judicature for Rajasthan, Bench
at Jaipur, heard the criminal appeal on merits but refrained from
passing a final order on the ground that the matter had not been
listed for hearing but only for consideration of the application
for suspension of sentence. We find no good ground and reason to
interfere with the impugned order, denying such relief to the
petitioner, at this stage.
However, as the appeal itself was taken up for hearing on
1

--- Page 624 ---
SLP(Crl.) No. 8613/2026
merits, we leave it open to the petitioner to file an appropriate
application to seek expeditious disposal of his appeal, as he has
been in custody for over eight years.
Subject to the above observations, the special leave petition
is dismissed.
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(DEEPAK GUGLANI)                             (PREETI SAXENA)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                         COURT MASTER (NSH)

“Both parties are present.  The matter was discussed
in detail and has been amicable settled between the
parties.  The appellant, Leorard Kishore, has agreed
to pay maintenance at the enhanced rate of Rs.6,500/-
(Rupees Six Thousand Five Hundred only) per month to

--- Page 640 ---
Respondent has agreed to waive all claims towards the
arrears of maintenance recoverable from the Appellant
up  to the date of settlement.  The  Respondent has
further agreed not to pursue or press any execution
petitions  or  other  proceedings  pending  before  the
respective courts for recovery of such arrears.  The
Respondent has also agreed that she shall not seek
enhancement of the maintenance amount for a period of
five  years  from  01.07.2026.  Accordingly,  she
undertakes not to file any application, petition or
proceeding seeking enhancement of maintenance during
the said period.”

disposed of.

               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CIVIL) DIARY NO(S). 30877/2026
[ARISING OUT OF IMPUGNED FINAL JUDGMENT AND ORDER DATED  27-11-2018
IN FA NO. 112/2018 PASSED BY THE HIGH COURT OF JHARKHAND AT RANCHI]
GOPAL  SAHU                                        PETITIONER(S)
                                VERSUS
PRABHA DEVI & ANR.                                 RESPONDENT(S)
IA NO. 193878/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING
IA  NO.  193886/2026  -  PERMISSION  TO  FILE  ADDITIONAL
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MRS. JUSTICE B.V. NAGARATHNA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE R. MAHADEVAN
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Mr. Kumar Shivam, AOR
                   Mr. Sameer Ranjan, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
We dismiss this Special Leave Petition on the ground
of delay as there is delay of 2611 days in filing this
Special Leave Petition. The reasons assigned for seeking
condonation  of  delay  are  nether  satisfactory  nor
sufficient  in  law  to  condone  the  same.  Hence,  the
application seeking condonation of delay is dismissed.
However, liberty is reserved to the petitioner to
proceed in accordance with law.

O R D E R
1. Having heard the learned counsel for the appellants and the
learned senior counsel, appearing for the respondent–company,
we find no grounds made out to interfere with the judgment and
order  dated  15.10.2024  passed  by  the  National  Consumer
Disputes  Redressal  Commission,  New  Delhi,  in  Consumer
Complaint No. 2159/2016. 
2. The appeal is, accordingly, dismissed.
3. The amount deposited by the appellants, pursuant to the order
dated 28.03.2025, along with interest accrued thereon, shall
be  disbursed  to  the  respondent  in  accordance  with  due
procedure. It is left open to the respondent to take necessary
further steps, consequent upon the dismissal of this appeal.
4. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
..........................J.
(SANJAY KUMAR)
..........................J.
(SANJEEV SACHDEVA)
NEW DELHI;
JULY 16, 2026.

ITEM NO.28               COURT NO.11               SECTION II-B
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CRIMINAL) DIARY NO(S). 28504/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  17-01-2012
in JCRLA No. 51/2002 passed by the High Court of Orissa at Cuttack]
GAGAN BEHERA & ANR.                                Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF ORISSA                                    Respondent(s)
IA No. 191702/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANOJ MISRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIJAY BISHNOI
For Petitioner(s) : Dr. Mrs. Vipin Gupta, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This special leave petition seeks to challenge the order dated
17.01.2012 passed by the Orissa High Court whereby the appeal of
the petitioners was partly allowed.  The conviction recorded under
Section 397 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 (IPC) was set aside but
the conviction under Section 396 IPC and life sentence therefore
were affirmed.
2. The office has reported this special leave petition to be
beyond time by 3060 days.  However, we have examined the matter on
merits also.  
3. Having gone through the materials placed on record including
1

--- Page 766 ---
the judgments of the courts below, we do not find any palpable
error in the view taken by the courts below particularly when the
prosecution witnesses 2 to 8 have substantiated the allegations.
Accordingly, we decline to entertain the special leave petition.
The same is, accordingly, dismissed.
4. However, dismissal of this petition shall not prejudice the
right of the petitioners to seek for remission of sentence as per
the State Policy.
5. All pending applications including application for condonation
of delay shall stand disposed of.
  (KAVITA PAHUJA)                               (SAPNA BANSAL)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

--- Page 767 ---

Court.
3. The Special Leave Petition is, accordingly, dismissed.
4. Pending application(s), if any, stands disposed of.

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  09-09-2025 
in CRMBA No. 30642/2025 passed by the High Court of Judicature at 
Allahabad]
FURKAN ALI                                         Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF U.P. & ANR.                               Respondent(s)
IA No. 40185/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
IA No. 126499/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K.V. VISWANATHAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARUN PALLI
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Asad Iqbal, Adv.
                   Mr. Chand Qureshi, AOR
                   Mr. Md.imran Siddiqui, Adv.
                   Mr. Faheem Uddin Md, Adv.
  
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Abhishek Saket, Adv.
                   Mr. Sudeep Kumar, AOR
                   Ms. Manisha, Adv.
                   Ms. Rupali, Adv.
                   Ms. Reya Paul, Adv.
                   Ms. Harshita Chawla, Adv.
  
                   Mr. Mujahid Ahmad, Adv.
Mr. Adil Muneer Andrabi, AOR
  
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This Special Leave Petition calls in question the correctness  
of  the  order  dated  09.09.2025  passed  by  the  High  Court  of  
Judicature at Allahabad in CRMBA No. 30642/2025. By the said order,  

--- Page 856 ---
the petitioner has been denied the benefit of regular bail in  
connection wit h  Case Crime No. 151 of 2025, registered for the  
offences under Sections 137(2),64(1) of BNS and Section 3/4 POCSO  
Act registered at P.S. Asmauli, District, Sambhal.
2. Heard Mr. Asad Iqbal, learned counsel for the pet itioner, and 
Mr. Abhishek Saket learned counsel appearing on behalf of the  
State, and Mr. Mujahid Ahmad, learned counsel for the  de facto 
complainant.
3. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner  
has undergone one year and one month in custody. Learned counsel  
for the petitioner draws attention of this Court to the deposition  
of P.W.1, i.e., the father of the prosecutorix, annexed with the  
counter-affidavit of the State. We have examined the same.
4. Having heard the learned counsel for the parties and perused  
the record, we are of the opinion that the petitioners should be  
enlarged on bail pending trial.
5. Accordingly, We direct that, pending trial, the petitioners  
shall  be  enlarged  on  bail  subject  to  the  satisfaction  and  
conditions as set by the Trial Court.
6. In view of the aforesaid, this Special Leave Petition is  
disposed of.
7. Pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.
(SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA)                           (MANOJ KUMAR)
   COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)

of the impugned order dated 16.01.2026 passed by the High Court of  

--- Page 858 ---
Punjab & Haryana at Chandigarh in CRM-M No. 3624/2024.
3. When the matter came up before us on 30.01.2026, we granted  
interim protection while issuing notice to the respondent. We also  
wanted the matter to be resolved through mediation. However, the  
parties have reported that the mediation has failed.

parties, we confirm the order of 30.01.2026. The petitioner shall,  
in the in the event of arrest, be enlarged on bail subject to the  
satisfaction of the concerned Investigating Officer in connection  
with the FIR No.87 dated 02.12.2023 registered for the offences  
under Sections 323, 406 and 498-A of IPC, registered at Police  
Station  PS  Women,  District  Police  Commissionerate,  Ludhiana,  
Punjab.

disposed of.

Ms. Aditi Soni, Adv.
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Having heard learned senior counsel for the petitioners, we
are not inclined to entertain this Writ Petition filed under
Article 32 of the Constitution.  The Writ Petition is, accordingly,
dismissed.
2. The petitioners, if so advised, may either approach the
1

--- Page 892 ---
National Green Tribunal or the jurisdictional High Court(s).  It
goes without saying that the forum to which the petitioners will
approach will make an endeavour to decide the matter expeditiously.

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 02-01-2026
in CRMP No.3756/2025 passed by the High Court of Chhatisgarh at
Bilaspur]
SAYYED RAAHIM ADNAN ALI                            Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF CHHATTISGARH & ANR.                       Respondent(s)
(IA No. 123152/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.)
 
Date : 16-07-2026 This matter was called for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s)  Dr. Harsh Pathak, Adv.
                   Ms. Shaveta Mahajan, AoR
                   Mr. Mohit Choubey, Adv.
                   Mr. Aditya Nema, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s)  Mr. Apoorv Shukla, AoR
    Ms. Prabhleen A. Shukla, Adv.
    Ms. Ilashri Gaur, Adv.
                   
          
                             O R D E R
Heard learned counsels for the parties.
2. The petitioner has assailed the order dated 02.01.2026 passed
by the High Court of Chhattisgarh at Bilaspur in CRMP No.3756 of
2025 by which, the petition filed by the petitioner for quashment
of entire criminal proceedings in Case No.243 of 2025, pending
before the Court of respondent no.2-Sub-Divisional Magistrate,

--- Page 896 ---
2
Abhanpur, District Raipur, Chhattisgarh under Sections 170, 126 and
135(3) of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, has been
dismissed. 
3. The core issue of the matter is whether the respondent no.2
could direct the petitioner to furnish a surety bond, when he had
already given a personal bond.
4. Learned  counsel  for  the  respondents  submitted  that  the
proceedings have finally been closed by order dated 10.11.2025 of
respondent no.2 and the petitioner was released on the third day
itself.
5. Learned counsel for the petitioner submitted that as the issue
has been closed, the Court may protect the petitioner from any
adverse impact of the present proceeding in future.
6. Having considered the matter in its entirety, the present
petition stands disposed of.
7. However,  we  observe  that  if  in  future,  based  on  these
proceedings which now stand concluded, the petitioner faces any
adverse impact, it shall be open for him to move before an
appropriate forum for redressal of such grievance, which shall be
considered by the forum/Court concerned, in accordance with law.
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand disposed of. 
(SAPNA BISHT)                                   (ANJALI PANWAR)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             ASSISTANT  REGISTRAR

                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No(s).11993/2025
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 10-03-2023
in CRA-S-849-SB-2005 (O&M) passed by the High Court of Punjab &
Haryana at Chandigarh]
CHAND SINGH                                        Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF HARYANA                               Respondent(s)
(IA No. 155843/2025 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.)
 
Date : 16-07-2026 This matter was called for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s)  Mr. Harmeet Singh Ruprah, AoR
                   Mr. Kanishk Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Karan Singh, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s)  Mr. Alok Sangwan, Sr. A.A.G.
                   Mr. Samar Vijay Singh, AoR
                   Mr. Sumit Kumar Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Rajat Sangwan, Adv.
                   Ms. Sabarni Som, Adv.
                   Mr. Harsh Mehla, Adv.
                   Mr. Vaibhav Yadav, Adv.
                   Mr. Rajat Sinha Roy, Adv.                       
          
                             O R D E R
Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and learned senior
Additional Advocate General for the respondent-State of Haryana.
2. The matter has been heard on merits.
3. The petitioner seeks interference in the impugned order dated

--- Page 902 ---
2
10.03.2023 passed by the High Court of Punjab and Haryana at
Chandigarh in CRA-S-849-SB-2005 (O&M) by which, the conviction and
sentence as awarded by the learned Special Judge, Rohtak, vide
judgment of conviction dated 07.02.2005 and order of sentence dated
09.02.2005 under Section 20 of the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic
Substances Act, 1985 (for short, the “NDPS Act”), has been upheld
by the High Court.
4. The petitioner is alleged to have been caught with 1 Kg. of
charas and upon trial, it has resulted in his conviction.
5. We have gone through the material on record and also given our
anxious thoughts to the submissions of the learned counsel for the
parties.
6. In our considered opinion, we do not find any infirmity in the
impugned order passed by the High Court warranting interference by
this Court.
7. Accordingly, the Special Leave Petition stands dismissed.
8. We further note that the petitioner had already been convicted
earlier in a case under the NDPS Act  and sentenced to undergo
twelve years of rigorous imprisonment, which order has attained
finality.
9. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(SAPNA BISHT)                                   (ANJALI PANWAR)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             ASSISTANT  REGISTRAR

For Respondent(s) : Ms.  Swathi H. Prasad, AOR
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Respondent Nos.1 to 3  have filed counter affidavit but
after the opportunity to file the same was already declined.
Hence, Registry to make a specific mention of this fact in
the  office  report  while  placing  the  matter  before  the
Hon'ble Court.
Notice sent for service on respondent No.10 has been
received back with the remarks “unclaimed”. Hence, service
is deemed to be complete on the said respondent in light of 
contd..

--- Page 916 ---
2
Judgment passed by this Hon’ble Court in K. Bhaskaran Vs.
Sankaran Vaidhyan Balan and Another, (1999) 7 Supreme Court
Cases 510 and order passed by the Registrar’s Court on

                   Mr. Bhupender Yadav, Adv.
                   Ms. Diva Singh, Adv.
                   Ms. Rashi Agnihotry, Adv.                   
For Respondent(s) : 
                   Mr. Anshul Narayan, Addl. Standing Counsel, Adv.
                   Mr. Anshuman Harsh, Adv.
                   Mr. Prem Prakash, AOR   
    Mr. Verma Ashish Kumar Madan Prasad, AOR
    Mr. Raghav Kumar, Adv.
    Mr. Saurav Kumar, Adv.
    Mr. M. Chandan Kanth Reddy, Adv.                
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Application seeking exemption from filing certified copy of
the impugned Order is allowed.
2. We heard the learned counsel appearing for the parties.
3. We fail to understand why the petitioner had to prefer an
application praying for anticipatory bail in a Complaint Case. All

--- Page 932 ---
2
that the Court has done is to issue summons. Police is no way
concerned in a Complaint Case. The apprehension that the petitioner
is likely to be arrested in pursuance of the Summons issued by the
Court is completely misconceived. 
4. It shall be open for the petitioner to avail appropriate steps
before the appropriate forum in accordance with law insofar as the
Order of issue of Summons is concerned. 
5. With the aforesaid, this petition stands disposed of.
6. Pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.
 (VISHAL ANAND)                                   (POOJA SHARMA)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                                 COURT MASTER (NSH)

                             O R D E R
IA No.102009 of 2026:
We have heard learned counsel for the petitioner and
learned standing counsel for the respondent-State. 
We dispose of this application by directing the
respondent-authorities  to  take  steps  to  present  the
petitioner  within  a  week’s  time  before  the  Regional
Institute  of  Medical  Sciences  for  the  purpose  of
undergoing an evaluation of the status of the Coronorary
Artery disease which the petitioner is stated to be
suffering from.
1

--- Page 1118 ---
It is needless to observe that on an evaluation of
the petitioner’s medical condition by the Department of
Coronary  Medicine  (Cardiology  Department),  appropriate
steps would be taken for further treatment, if any.

                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  19-12-2025 
in SBCRMTBA No. 16878/2025 passed by the High Court of Judicature 
for Rajasthan at Jaipur]
PUSHPENDRA SINGH                                   Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF RAJASTHAN                             Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
IA No. 189249/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN REFILING /  CURING THE 
DEFECTS
IA No. 189243/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
IA No. 189247/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL 
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Soumya Srivastava, Adv.
                   Mr. Aryan Dev Pandey, Adv.
    Mr. Arnab Kumar Mondal, Adv.
                   Mr. Ranjan Nikhil Dharnidhar, AoR
  
  
For Respondent(s) :Ms. Sansriti Pathak, A.A.G.
    Ms. Shagufta Khan, Adv.
                   Mr. Saurabh Rajpal, AoR
  
  
                           O R D E R
Heard learned counsel for the parties.
2. Delay condoned.
3. The petitioner is accused in FIR No.274/2023, registered at  
Police Station Ramganj Mandi, District Kota Rural Rajasthan for the  
offence(s) under Section 8/21 of the NDPS Act. 

--- Page 1134 ---
4. The allegation against the petitioner is that from the car of  
which he was driving, 938 grams of heroin were recovered from the  
spare tire compartment in the boot of the car.
5. Learned counsel for the petitioner submitted that there is  
delay of sending the sample for forensic report. It was further  
contended that the petitioner was just a driver and the owner of  
the car was responsible for the said material found. It was further  
contended that the co-accused who was travelling in the car and one  
Saudan Singh were the persons responsible for the narcotic found in  
the boot of the car.
6. Learned counsel for the State submits that the petitioner  
cannot plead ignorance as he was the driver and the material found  
in the boot cannot be said to have been kept there without his  
knowledge. It was further contended that there cannot be any parity  
with the other co-accused at this stage as it was the petitioner  
who was driving the car carrying the narcotics. It was submitted  
that the quantity of almost one kg and that too of heroin being  
recovered, the Court may take a serious note of the prevailing  
ground conditions and also the conduct of the petitioner.
7. Having considered the matter in its entirety, we are not  
inclined to allow the prayer for bail to the petitioner, for the  
present.
8. Accordingly, the Special Leave Petition stands dismissed.
9. Pending applications, if any, shall stand disposed of.
  (SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA)                      (ANJALI PANWAR)
    COURT MASTER (SH)                          ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

SHUBHANK GUPTA @ SHUBHU                            Petitioner(s)

DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Ajay Awasthi, Adv.
                   Mr. Rishi Jaiswal, AoR
                   Mr. Kunal Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Ayush Upadhyay, Adv.
  
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Vinayak Sharma, Standing Counsel, Adv.
                   Mr. Ravinder Kumar Yadav, AoR
                   Mr. Yashvardhan Shah, Adv.
  
  
                           O R D E R
Heard learned counsel for the parties.
2. The petitioner is accused in Crime No.02/2025 registered at  
Police Station AJAK, District - Rajnandgaon (C.G.) for the offence  
punishable under Sections 296, 351(2), 3(5) of BNS and Section 3(1)
(g) and 3(2)(Va) of SC & ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989. 
3. The basic allegation against the petitioner is that he used  

--- Page 1136 ---
abusive language and the same was also caught on the CCTV installed  
at his house.
4. The complainant has also previously lodged complaints over the  
same dispute between the parties against the petitioner.
5. This court earlier on 10.04.2026 while issuing notice had  
granted interim protection to the petitioner subject to cooperating  
with the investigation. However, in the counter affidavit filed on  
behalf of the state of Chhattisgarh on 10 th of May 2026, a  
categorical statement has been made that the petitioner has not  
been cooperating. No reply has been filed to the said categorical  
assertion by the State in the counter affidavit.
6. Having considered the matter, we find that the petitioner does  
not deserve the privilege of anticipatory bail in the peculiar  
facts and circumstances of the present case.
7. For the reasons aforesaid, the Special Leave Petition stands  
dismissed.
8. However, if the petitioner appears before the Court below  
within one week from today and seeks bail, the same shall be  
considered by the Trial Court on its own merits and in accordance  
with law without being prejudiced by the present order.
9. The interim protection granted earlier to the petitioner  
stands withdrawn.
10. Pending applications, if any, shall stand disposed of.
  (SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA)                      (ANJALI PANWAR)

                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No(s).  9234/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  25-03-2026 
in MCRC No. 12200/2026 passed by the High Court of Madhya Pradesh 
Principal Seat at Jabalpur]
SANJAY AWASTHI                                     Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF MADHYA PRADESH                            Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
IA No. 152619/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 13-07-2026 This matter was called for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) :Dr. Rupesh Singh, Adv.
                   Mrs. Smriti Singh, Adv.
                   Mrs. Jyoti Nagar, Adv.
                   Mr. Siddharth Yadav, Adv.
                   Mr. Satya Veer Singh, AoR
  
  
For Respondent(s) : 
                           O R D E R
Heard learned counsel for the parties.
2. The petitioner seeks bail in connection with Crime No.10/2024 
registered at Police Station, Kotwali, District Chhatarpur (M.P.)  
for the offences punishable under Sections 353, 332, 294, 323, 506  
& 34 of IPC . The accusation against the petitioner is that he had  
assaulted  the  Doctor  who  declared  a  patient  brought  by  the 
petitioner to the Hospital upon examination, as dead. It was  
further alleged that the petitioner along with others had beaten up  

--- Page 1144 ---
and abused the complainant Doctor and then went back and came with  
others and the Doctor was assaulted which led to physical injury  
including blood coming out from the right ear and also from the  
mouth of the complainant.
3. Learned counsel for the petitioner submitted that whatever  
happened was unfortunate and the same was in the heat of the  
moment, as the patient of the petitioner has died. However, he  
submitted that the situation was not alarming and only some  
skirmish occurred and the matter is two years old, hence, the  
prayer for anticipatory bail be sympathetically considered.
4. Learned counsel for the State submits that the situation is  
serious as a Doctor who goes beyond his professional call to save a  
person, just because he is unable to revive a person who is brought  
dead is assaulted in a brutal manner by the petitioner.
5. Having considered the matter in its entirety, we do not find  
any  ground  made  out  for  grant  of  anticipatory  bail  to  the  
petitioner in the facts and circumstances of the present case.
6. Accordingly, the Special Leave Petition stands dismissed.
7. Pending applications, if any, shall stand disposed of.
  (SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA)                      (ANJALI PANWAR)
    COURT MASTER (SH)                          ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No(s).  9429/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  01-05-2026 
in CRMM No. 4698/2026 passed by the High Court of Punjab & Haryana 
at Chandigarh]
SHANE ALAM @ SEM @ SANE ALAM                       Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF HARYANA                                   Respondent(s)
Date : 13-07-2026 This petition was called for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Akshay Sahay, AoR
                   Mr. Harish Chhabra, Adv.
  
  
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Vikramaditya Chouhan, A.A.G.
                   Mr. Samar Vijay Singh, AoR
                   Mr. Sachin Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Deepanshu Malik, Adv.
                   Ms. Sabarni Som, Adv.
                   Mr. Aman Dev Sharma, Adv.
  
  
                           O R D E R
Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and the learned  
counsel for the state of Haryana.
2. The petitioner is accused of killing the daughter of the  
informant.
3. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that he is totally  
unconnected with the crime and the husband of the deceased is the  
main culprit, but the police in connivance with the husband has let  
him off and implicated the petitioner. It was submitted that there  

--- Page 1146 ---
is no recovery of mobile and other material at his instance, and  
the Doctor who has conducted the postmortem has opined that the  
strangulation which led to the death of the deceased could have  
been caused by  Taviz.  Thus, it was submitted that as per the  
prosecution story, the strangulation being by a dupatta, and the  
Doctor opining that it was by a  Taviz, the case as far as the  
petitioner is concerned against stands demolished. It was further  
submitted that the petitioner has been in custody since one year  
and nine months.
4. Learned counsel for the State submitted that at the instance  
of the petitioner, recovery has been made of his shirt, a rubber  
band, a dupatta, mobile phone of the deceased and also Taviz. It 
was  further  contended  that  there  is  also  evidence  that  the  
petitioner  was  well  known  to  the  deceased  and  as  per  the  
prosecution, he had some relationship with her. Further, the CDR  
indicates that the deceased and the petitioner were at the same  
location.
5. Having considered the matter and taking note of the material  
on record, we are not inclined to allow the prayer for bail to the  
petitioner, for the present.
6. Accordingly, the Special Leave Petition, stands dismissed.
7. Pending applications, if any, shall stand disposed of.
  (SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA)                      (ANJALI PANWAR)
    COURT MASTER (SH)                          ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

IA No. 180039/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING

DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 14-07-2026 This matter was called for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) :Ms. Aishwarya Bhati, A.S.G.
                   Mr. Tanmaya Agarwal, AoR
                   Ms. Aditi Agarwal, Adv.
                   Mr. Udit Bhardwaj, Adv.
  
  
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Anurag Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Pranav Sarthi, AoR
                   Ms. Neelakshi, Adv.
  
  
                            O R D E R
Ms. Aishwarya Bhati, learned ASG, submits that the Court may  
grant a short adjournment for the reason that, as per the decision  
taken  by  the  Union  of  India,  Ministry  of  Personnel,  Public  
Grievances and Pensions, Department of Personnel and Training  
addressed to the Chief Secretary, Government of Uttar Pradesh vide  
letter dated 27.04.2026, issued after passing the impugned order,  

--- Page 1152 ---
wherein taking into account the facts and circumstances of the  
present case the respondents are deemed confirmed in the IAS as  
special case and now, as a consequence they would be called upon to  
give their consent in terms thereof. 
2. Having regard to the aforesaid, list on 14.08.2026.
3. In the meantime, learned counsel for the private respondents  
submits that they would not press the contempt petition(s) filed by

--- Page 1158 ---
3. Having regard to the aforesaid, the matter is referred to the

4. However, whenever the petitioner is required to be physically  
present in Delhi for mediation, the petitioner will be entitled to  
reimbursement of her cost towards traveling, boarding and lodging,  
which we quantify to Rs.10,000/- (Rupees Ten Thousand) per visit.  
The same shall be transferred into the account of the petitioner by  
the respondent at least four days prior to the date fixed for her  
appearance for each visit.
5. Learned Mediator is requested to submit a report within two  
months thereafter.
6. List on 07.10.2026.
7. To facilitate the Mediation, all inter se pending proceedings 
between the parties shall remain stayed.
8. After  the  order  was  dictated,  learned  counsel  for  the  
petitioner submitted that in a collateral proceeding where interim  
maintenance has been granted to the petitioner, the respondent has  
not paid such maintenance for the last over three months. We make  
it clear that the respondent is obliged to pay the said interim  
maintenance and the up to date payment be made within one week from  
today.

               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No(s).  9461-
9462/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  24-03-2025  
in CRLREVP No. 667/2023 02-04-2026 in EXCRL No. 150/2022 passed by  
the]
SNIGDHA MEHRA                                      Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
RAGHAV MEHRA                                       Respondent(s)
IA No. 141424/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING
IA No. 141421/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT
IA No. 141422/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 15-07-2026 This matter was called for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Osama Suhail, Adv.
                   Mr. Samama Suhail, Adv.
                   Ms. Aishwarya Raj, Adv.
                   Ms. Antara Chaudhury, Adv.
                   Mr. Abhishek Sharma, AoR
  
  
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Pritish Sabharwal, Adv.
                   Mr. Amrendra Kumar Mehta, AoR
                   Ms. Shweta Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Shiv Chopra, Adv.
  
  
                            O R D E R
Heard learned counsel for the petitioner.
2. We notice that despite there being a subsisting orders of the  
Family Court for the respondent to pay Rs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five  
Lakhs) per month as maintenance, only Rs. 30,00,000/- (Rupees  
Thirty Lakhs) has been paid. Though, as per the calculation made by  

--- Page 1170 ---
the learned counsel for the petitioner, as of today, the arrears  
would be about Rs. 7,35,00,000/- (Rupees Seven Crores Thirty Five  
Lakhs) out of which only Rs. 30,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty Lakhs) has  
been  paid  and  thus  presently,  the  balance  outstanding  is  
approximately Rs. 7,00,00,000/- (Rupees Seven Crores).
3. Be that as it may, we find that on record there is a  
calculation made by the Nazir of the Court concerned indicating  
that there was dues of Rs. 4,85,00,000/- (Rupees Four Crores Eighty  
Five Lakhs) as reflected in the order dated 06.02.2026 by the  
Family Court.
4. Having regard to the aforesaid, we direct the respondent to  
pay a sum of  Rs. 3,00,00,000/- (Rupees Three Crores) to the  
petitioner, within six weeks from today.
5. List on 10.09.2026 at the top of the board.
6. After the order was passed, learned counsel for the respondent  
prays that this Court may grant eight weeks time to pay the amount  
to the petitioner.
7. We accede to the request. Accordingly, let the amount of Rs.  
3,00,00,000/- (Rupees Three Crores) be paid to the petitioner by  
the respondent within the said period.
(SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA)                       (ANJALI PANWAR)
   COURT MASTER (SH)                          ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

Counter affidavit, on behalf of respondent Nos. 1.4, if any,
be filed on or before 30.7.2026
Counter affidavit, on behalf of respondent No. 4, if any, be
filed on or before 30.7.2026, as last opportunity. 
As per the office report, Ld. Counsel for the petitioner has
to file requisite spare copies for issuance of notice to Respondent
Nos. 3.1 & 3.2.
However, today Mr. Siddharth Sati, Ld. Counsel appearing on
behalf of Ms. Vipasha Singh, Ld. Advocate-on-Record has entered
appearance on behalf of Respondent Nos. 3.1 and 3.2 and submits
that on 6.6.2025 vakalatnama on behalf of Respondent Nos. 3.1 and
3.2 has already been filed vide e-filing No. EDSCIN01-53620-2025.
Registry to verify the same and update the records.
Ld. Counsel further submits that Respondent Nos. 3.1 and 3.2
