DINESH PRASAD                                      Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
NAGESHWAR SAH & ANR.                               Respondent(s)
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANOJ MISRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIJAY BISHNOI
For Petitioner(s) :By Courts Motion, AOR
                   
                   Mr. Arpit Sharma, Adv.
                   Ms. Rangoli Seth, AOR
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Amit Sinha, Adv.
                   Mr. Pawan Kumar Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Guru Bandhu, Adv.
                   Mr. Rohit Kumar, Adv.
                   Mr. Dharmendra Kumar Sinha, AOR
                                      
                   Mr. Anando Mukherjee, AOR
                   Mr. Dev Aaryan, Adv.
                                      
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. By order dated 22.05.2026, the amount deposited by
the appellant was to be released in favour of Respondent
No.1 (the complainant). The office has submitted a report
that there is no direction in respect of release of the
interest earned on the said deposit.
2. In view of the above, we deem it appropriate to
clarify that the amount of interest accrued on such
deposit shall also be released in favour of Respondent
No.1.
3. M.A. is accordingly disposed of.
  (KAVITA PAHUJA)                               (SAPNA BANSAL)

               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CIVIL) DIARY NO(S). 34993/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  24-04-2025
in CWP No. 6334/2025 passed by the High Court of Himachal Pradesh
at Shimla]
VIJAY KUMAR                                        Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF HIMACHAL PRADESH & ORS.               Respondent(s)
IA No. 194631/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANOJ MISRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIJAY BISHNOI
For Petitioner(s) : Ms. Vandana Sharma Bhandari, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner was denied relief on the ground that
he had petitioned the court with an inordinate delay of
almost 9 years.  The special leave petition too is reported
to be beyond time by 314 days.
2. Having regard to the above, we do not find a good
reason to interfere with the impugned order.  Though as a
matter of compassion, we deem it appropriate to condone the
delay of 314 days in filing the special leave petition.
3. Subject  to  above,  the  special  leave  petition  is
dismissed.
4. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of.
(KAVITA PAHUJA)                                 (SAPNA BANSAL)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI

                   Mr. Vidya Bhushan Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Randhir Kumar Ojha, AOR                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We are satisfied that no writ petition under Article 32 of the
Constitution of India is maintainable for the reliefs as sought in
the writ petition. The petitioner, if so advised, may approach the
High Court with an application for expeditious disposal of the
pending petition.

                   Mr. Aakash Nandolia, AOR
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Learned Counsel for the petitioner states that the matter has
been amicably settled, and thata formal Memorandum of Understanding
(MoU) dated 14.07.2026 has been executed between the parties.
2. To that end, he had filed an Interlocutory Application (I.A.)
yesterday to place on record that settlement and its particulars.
However, the I.A. has not been listed today. Be that as it may, a
photostat copy of the MoU has been handed over in Court, and the
same is taken on record.
3. In view of the MoU, the Transfer Petition is dismissed as
withdrawn, with liberty to the aggrieved party to seek revival of
the present transfer petition in the event of any breach of the
terms and conditions of the MoU.

ITEM NO.1720               COURT NO.17               SECTION XI-B
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Transfer Petition(s)(Civil)  No(s).  833-834/2024
UNION OF INDIA                                     Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
BIJY PAUL & ORS.                                   Respondent(s)
IA No. 40992/2026 - SUBSTITUTED SERVICE
 
Date : 15-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SATISH CHANDRA SHARMA
                           [IN CHAMBER]
For Petitioner(s) : Dr. N. Visakamurthy, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : Mr. Nishe Rajen Shonker, AOR
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. IA No. 40992/2026 is an application to serve summon upon the
respondent  by  substituted  service  through  their  counsel
representing in writ petition before High Court.
2. The cause title of the petition reflects that large number of
respondents are residing in United Kingdom and therefore, such a
request of Union of India cannot be allowed.  There is a procedure
to serve the respondents residing abroad and the Union of India has
to  follow  the  aforesaid  procedure.   Resultantly,  I.A.  no.
40992/2026 is rejected.
3. Learned counsel for Union of India is granted four weeks’ time
to  take  appropriate  steps  in  the  matter  for  serving  the
respondents.
 (KANIKA TAYAL)                                  (KOMAL)
COURT MASTER (SH)                     COURT MASTER (NSH)

List the matter again before this Court on 17.08.2026.
Copy of this Record of Proceedings be furnished to the
concerned  learned  Advocate-on-Record  for  information  and
compliance.
                                            GRACY L. BAWITLUNG
                                                  Registrar
